title: podman does not perserve ownership of files when adding archives
body:
When using ADD in a Dockerfile with a tarball it fails to preserve ownership from the archive the same way docker does. This only started recently. It should restore the ownership of files to the owner stored in the tarball so if I archive a file belonging to bob and then extract it using ADD it should also belong to bob or the same UID in the resulting image.

See: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/3218

The only workaround at this point might be to copy the archive into the docker image then extract and rm it instead.

comments.body: Please provide additional details - what Podman version are you using, and a full `podman info`.

A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I believe this is fixed in the latest code.  There has been a major update of how copy/add are handled in buildah which has been merged into podman.  If you still find issues with this, please open an issue with buildah bud, since it would have to be fixed there first.
